title: Time-ordered CatBoost CTR model on raw categorical crosses
summary: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and categorical combinations are designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and categorical combinations are designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage. This dataset has high-cardinality categories (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`) and natural order within each driver-race sequence, which is exactly where ordered CTRs can outperform manual frequency encoding.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoostClassifier sorted by `Year, Race, Driver, LapNumber` with `has_time=True`, feeding raw categoricals plus a few numeric core fields. Let CatBoost build combos like `Driver×Race`, `Driver×Compound`, `Race×Compound`, `Driver×Stint`, and blend its OOF predictions with the current tree baseline rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Adds a genuinely different categorical signal family and may capture driver/race/compound interaction priors more cleanly than hand-engineered counts.
risk: If row order is wrong, CTRs become noisy; CatBoost may underperform if numeric tyre-state features still dominate.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
